summary: Tested AWS Cognito + DynamoDB auth foundation. Backend: 12/15 pytest tests passed. Critical: Login flow is broken on Cognito side (returns 400 'Invalid request parameters' for ALL login attempts — wrong creds, unverified, etc.), and Resend with non-existent email returns 200 instead of expected 404. Frontend: All 6 auth pages render & wire up correctly (signup→verify redirect with email param, code sanitization, no demo hint on login, forgot→reset redirect, password mismatch validation, ProtectedRoute redirect). Floating 'Talk to OraOne' widget NOT detected on marketing homepage — possible regression.

critical.issue: Returns 400 'Invalid request parameters.' for BOTH wrong credentials AND unverified users. Expected 401/403 respectively. Root cause: Cognito App Client `7s9605iohk40otqpmt33v3kmpe` likely does NOT have the `ALLOW_USER_PASSWORD_AUTH` (or equivalent USER_PASSWORD_AUTH) explicit auth flow enabled — `cognito-idp.initiate_auth(AuthFlow='USER_PASSWORD_AUTH')` raises InvalidParameterException when that flow is disabled. This means END-TO-END LOGIN IS COMPLETELY BROKEN — even with valid verified creds, the user can never log in via this API.

With a non-existent email, returns 200 'A new verification code has been sent.' instead of the spec-required 404 'No account found with this email.' Either (a) Cognito's resend_confirmation_code no longer throws UserNotFoundException (silent anti-enumeration at AWS level) — code in auth_service.py looks correct but Cognito isn't raising. Need to verify boto3 behaviour or add an explicit DDB pre-check.

minor.issue: Error mapping: when wrong credentials are sent and the email is unknown to Cognito, the API returns 400 InvalidParameterException-mapped message. Once USER_PASSWORD_AUTH is enabled, expect Cognito to return UserNotFoundException (404) or NotAuthorizedException (401). The spec wants a unified 401 'Invalid email or password.' to prevent enumeration — consider remapping UserNotFoundException -> 401 for the /login route specifically.

integration_issues.issue: Floating 'Talk to OraOne' widget was NOT detected on https://.../ — no element with data-testid matching widget/chat/floating, no aria-label or text containing 'Talk to OraOne' found. This may be a regression OR the widget is lazily mounted behind cookie consent. Visually no FAB visible in screenshot.

action_items: URGENT — Enable `ALLOW_USER_PASSWORD_AUTH` (ExplicitAuthFlows) on Cognito App Client 7s9605iohk40otqpmt33v3kmpe in user pool ap-south-2_FL0C37jsC, OR switch backend to SRP auth. Without this, NO USER CAN EVER LOG IN. Verify via AWS console: User Pools → ap-south-2_FL0C37jsC → App integration → App clients → 7s9605iohk40otqpmt33v3kmpe → Authentication flows must include ALLOW_USER_PASSWORD_AUTH.

Resend endpoint anti-enumeration: confirm whether Cognito is silently succeeding for unknown users (in which case the spec needs updating) or add an explicit existence check (DynamoDB `oraone-users` lookup by email index) before calling resend.

Verify the marketing-page 'Talk to OraOne' floating widget renders — likely the FloatingWidget component was descoped or the data-testid was removed during the auth refactor. Add data-testid='floating-widget' to make it testable.
Add a UserNotFoundException -> 401 'Invalid email or password.' override inside the /login service path (currently maps to 404) to match the spec's anti-enumeration intent.
critical_code_review_comments: auth_service.login() in app/services/auth_service.py calls initiate_auth(AuthFlow='USER_PASSWORD_AUTH') — this REQUIRES the App Client to have ALLOW_USER_PASSWORD_AUTH listed in ExplicitAuthFlows. Smoke test in the original spec apparently never actually attempted a real login, otherwise this would have been caught.

AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID / AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY in /app/backend/.env are EMPTY. boto3 must be falling back to IAM role / instance profile / ~/.aws/credentials. If running in a fresh container without those credentials, ALL Cognito + DDB calls will fail. This needs a clear deployment runbook.

Cognito error map maps InvalidParameterException -> 400 'Invalid request parameters.' — this masked the underlying USER_PASSWORD_AUTH disabled error and made debugging slow. Consider logging the raw Cognito error code+message at WARN level so backend logs show what AWS actually said.

server.py still imports bcrypt/jwt/secrets and defines hash_password, create_access_token, get_current_user etc that ARE NO LONGER USED by any auth route. Dead code — remove to avoid confusion (the dashboard/agents/leads routes do use get_current_user which still works against the legacy `users` Mongo collection that no longer gets populated). This is BROKEN: every existing /api/dashboard/overview, /api/agents, /api/leads endpoint now uses a Mongo-based auth that will never find a user, because login no longer writes to db.users. Effectively the dashboard backend is decoupled from the new Cognito auth and CANNOT BE USED.

/api/auth/me on success returns UserProfile from DynamoDB via user_service.get_user_profile(user_id) — but /api/dashboard/overview etc. use a different `get_current_user` that queries Mongo. These two auth systems do not bridge. The dashboard will 401 even for a freshly-logged-in Cognito user.
test_credentials: No test credentials in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. AWS access keys in /app/backend/.env are EMPTY — boto3 must rely on container-level IAM. Throwaway email pattern used: qa-{timestamp}@example.com. The throwaway Cognito user created during testing CANNOT be verified (no inbox access) and CANNOT be logged in (USER_PASSWORD_AUTH not enabled).
seed_data_creation: Created one throwaway Cognito user per backend run (email qa-{timestamp}@example.com). User remains in UNCONFIRMED state — no DDB row written until first successful login. Recommend periodic cleanup in Cognito console.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent enables ALLOW_USER_PASSWORD_AUTH on the Cognito App Client, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_cognito_auth.py. The two Login failure tests should then return 401 (wrong creds) and 403 (unverified). The Resend non-existent test may still fail if Cognito's anti-enumeration is at the AWS layer — confirm with main agent whether to keep that assertion or relax it. Also verify the FloatingWidget on /. Also: the dashboard endpoints (/api/agents, /api/leads, /api/dashboard/overview) use the OLD Mongo-based get_current_user and are now completely orphaned from the new Cognito auth — main agent must port these to use the Cognito JWT middleware before they're usable.
rca_of_the_issue: Login 400 'Invalid request parameters.': boto3 cognito-idp.initiate_auth(AuthFlow='USER_PASSWORD_AUTH', ...) raises ClientError code=InvalidParameterException when the app client's ExplicitAuthFlows list does not include ALLOW_USER_PASSWORD_AUTH (or ALLOW_USER_PASSWORD_AUTH+ALLOW_REFRESH_TOKEN_AUTH). Mitigation: in AWS Console → Cognito → ap-south-2_FL0C37jsC → App client 7s9605iohk40otqpmt33v3kmpe → Edit Authentication flows → tick 'ALLOW_USER_PASSWORD_AUTH'. Alternatively (more secure) switch to USER_SRP_AUTH and use warrant or pycognito for SRP. Reproduction: POST /api/auth/login {email:any, password:any} -> 400 'Invalid request parameters.' instead of the expected 401.
